title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

The world map displays the geographic distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, categorized into two distinct groups: those eligible for support, shown in teal, and those not eligible, shown in light beige. The teal shading, indicating eligibility, covers a contiguous block of nations across Sub-Saharan Africa, extending from the western coast through the central region to the eastern horn and down to the southern tip, excluding only a few specific territories. In Asia, the eligible category includes countries in the Middle East such as Yemen, extends across South Asia including Pakistan, India, Nepal, and Bangladesh, and reaches into Southeast Asia with Myanmar and Papua New Guinea also shaded teal. North Korea is similarly marked as eligible. In contrast, the light beige color representing ineligibility dominates the maps of North America, South America, Europe, and Australia. Within Africa, nations in the north along the Mediterranean coast and specific countries in the south appear in the beige category. The visual pattern shows a clear concentration of supported countries in the lower-income regions of Africa and parts of Asia, while higher-income regions across the Americas, Europe, and Oceania fall outside the support criteria.
